goal: Build a CP-01-equivalent cell using target_source='spline' (CPU-only deterministic Cartesian waypoint controller) instead of cuRobo. Validates the spline mode for users without GPU/cuRobo. Slower cycle but reproducible.
thoughts:
Six spline-mode specifics for CP-40 (extends CP-01 — different target_source):
1) target_source='spline' — uses scipy.CubicSpline-interpolated 6-waypoint Cartesian trajectory. CPU-only, deterministic.
2) Same scene as CP-01: Franka + 4 cubes on conveyor + bin behind. Tests that handler dispatches correctly for non-curobo modes.
3) Spline mode doesn't support drop_targets (yet) — uses single drop_target or destination_path bbox center. CP-40 uses default (no drop_target arg).
4) spline_waypoint_dt default 1.5s × 6 segments = 9s cycle (vs cuRobo's ~3-5s). Slower but always works.
5) Per CP-01's verified_metrics: spline gets 3/4 cubes_in_bin_mean. So function-gate may fail on Cube_4. Acceptable for demo.
6) Industrial use: CPU-only deployments (no GPU available), reproducible cycle-time validation, sim2real demos.
code:
# DomeLight + Ground + Cell + Table
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Cell", prim_type="Xform")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[1.0, 0.5, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka_panda",
    dest_path="/World/Franka",
    position=[0, 0, 0.75],
    orientation=[0.7071068, 0, 0, 0.7071068],
)

create_conveyor(
    prim_path="/World/ConveyorBelt",
    position=[0.0, 0.4, 0.78],
    size=[3.0, 0.4, 0.05],
    surface_velocity=[0.2, 0, 0],
)

for i, x in enumerate([-1.4, -1.15, -0.9, -0.65]):
    path = f"/World/Cube_{i+1}"
    create_prim(prim_path=path, prim_type="Cube", position=[x, 0.4, 0.835], size=0.05)
    for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
        apply_api_schema(prim_path=path, schema_name=api)

bulk_set_attribute(
    prim_paths=[f"/World/Cube_{i+1}" for i in range(4)],
    attr="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold",
    value=0.0,
)
for path in [f"/World/Cube_{i+1}" for i in range(4)]:
    apply_physics_material(prim_path=path, material_name="rubber")

create_bin(prim_path="/World/Bin", position=[0, -0.4, 0.75], size=[0.3, 0.3, 0.15])

add_proximity_sensor(sensor_path="/World/PickSensor", position=[0.4, 0.4, 0.835], size=[0.06, 0.06, 0.06])

# Spline controller — CPU-only, deterministic
setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path="/World/Franka",
    target_source="spline",
    sensor_path="/World/PickSensor",
    belt_path="/World/ConveyorBelt",
    source_paths=[f"/World/Cube_{i+1}" for i in range(4)],
    destination_path="/World/Bin",
)
extension_notes: CP-40 uses target_source='spline' — CPU-only deterministic alternative to cuRobo. Per CP-01's benchmark_vs_alternatives, spline gets 3/4 cubes_in_bin_mean (vs cuRobo's 4/4). Cycle ~9s/cube (vs cuRobo ~5s). Trade-off: deterministic + CPU but slower + lower delivery.
failure_modes: Spline doesn't support drop_targets (yet) — drops at single destination_path bbox center.
scipy.CubicSpline interpolation can produce trajectories that brush table — without scene-collision check (cuRobo has it, spline doesn't).
Slower cycle means cubes pile up on conveyor if input rate exceeds robot's pick rate.
